newsSince: Material: On June 17, 2026 Jabil reported Q3 FY2026 (revenue $8.8B +12% YoY, core EPS $3.16 +24%, beat consensus) and raised FY26 guidance to ~$35B revenue (from $34B), core EPS $12.70, FCF >$1.4B. AI-related revenue now expected ~$13.6B in FY26 (+50% YoY, $500M above March outlook), Cloud & Data Center $10.9B (+47%). Stock jumped ~10% post-print toward the top of its 52-week range.

sources.label: Jabil Q3 FY2026 slides: AI +50%, outlook raised (Investing.com)
Jabil lifts outlook on AI data-center demand (ts2.tech)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: June 17 beat-and-raise (FY26 to ~$35B, AI rev ~$13.6B +50%) is a clear fundamental positive (+2) confirming central-node franchise quality. But the prior 'crowded/rerated' call holds: stock +10% post-print near 52-wk highs erodes residual alpha, so no rank move on this page.
verdicts.bull: AI revenue ~$13.6B (+50% YoY, $500M above March), Cloud/DC $10.9B (+47%); FY26 raised to ~$35B; grid-to-rack power and liquid cooling central node.
verdicts.neutral: Strong print, but the thesis here is residual alpha vs passives, and the stock has already rerated hard.
verdicts.bear: +54% YTD, spot near 52-wk high and well above PT; crowded/rerated leaves limited residual alpha on this lens.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued AI data-center capacity ramp and FY27 guidance.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded, rerated valuation; AI-capex digestion / customer concentration.
